methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
MightyFly is a Series A stage drone and autonomous systems company founded by Manal Habib, having raised $10M to date. The comparable universe consists of eight private companies in the Drones & Autonomous sector, including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No public comps exist in this narrowly defined subsector.

The valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method: stage-progression analysis. This approach applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $10M total raised proxy, reflecting typical early-stage progression mechanics between Series A and subsequent funding rounds. This method yields an implied valuation of $20M. A peer-median approach was not viable due to insufficient narrow-stage comparable data; while five same-sector peers exist, fewer than three companies share identical stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation-tier characteristics necessary for reliable median benchmarking.

The triangulated valuation range is $17M (low) to $23M (high), with a median of $20M. This narrow band reflects reliance on a single methodological pillar rather than robust triangulation across independent approaches. Key uncertainties include the absence of directly comparable Series A transactions in the subsector, which limits conviction in stage-progression assumptions, and the early-stage nature of the company, where typical revenue or unit economics data remain immaterial to traditional DCF frameworks. Validation of any valuation in this range would require deeper diligence into MightyFly's technology differentiation, capital efficiency, and path to the next funding event.
